class.name: NeighbourhoodServer
class.brief_description: Node that provides fast spatial neighbourhood queries over subscribed [Node2D] instances.
class.description: [NeighbourhoodServer] maintains a uniform spatial grid that is rebuilt at a configurable interval during physics processing. [Node2D] instances register themselves via [method subscribe] and can be queried by world position using [method get_next] and [method get_all].

method.name: subscribe
method.description: Registers [param node] with a collision [param layer] bitmask and an arbitrary [param data] payload. If [param node] is already subscribed its entry is overwritten.

method.name: unsubscribe
method.description: Removes [param node] from the server so it is no longer returned by spatial queries. Safe to omit if the node is about to be freed — stale entries are cleaned up automatically on the next refresh.

method.name: get_next
method.description: Returns the data payload of the nearest subscriber to [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. Returns [code]null[/code] if no match is found. [param max_distance] limits the search radius in world units. Use [code]0.0[/code] for unlimited range.

method.name: get_all
method.description: Returns an [Array] of data payloads for all subscribers within [param max_distance] of [param position] whose layer matches [param layer_mask]. Unlike [method get_next], [param max_distance] must be set explicitly — passing [code]0.0[/code] returns an empty array.

members.member: Width and height of each spatial grid cell in world units. Smaller values improve query precision at the cost of more cells to manage; larger values reduce overhead but increase the number of subscribers checked per query. A good starting point is the average interaction radius of your queries.
Minimum time in seconds between grid rebuilds. Set to [code]0.0[/code] to rebuild every physics frame. Higher values reduce CPU cost at the expense of query freshness.

When [code]true[/code] (default), subscriber positions are read via [method Node2D.get_global_position]. Set to [code]false[/code] to use [method Node2D.get_position] (local space), which is significantly faster when all subscribers share the same parent and no global transform traversal is needed.
